title: M11 test support: builder extensions
analysis:
The reify-test-support crate has four modules: builders (TopologyTemplateBuilder, CompiledModuleBuilder, expression helpers), values (scalar/ID constructors), fixtures (bracket/parent-child modules), and mocks. The task requires extending it in four areas:

1. **Complex value constructors** — values.rs only has scalar helpers (mm, deg, etc.). Tests constructing List, Set, Map, Enum, Option, Field, Lambda values must use verbose manual construction. We need convenience constructors.

2. **Extended literal() and expression builders** — literal() panics on M5 types (Enum, List, Set, Map, Option, Lambda, Field). CompiledExpr has no named constructors for Conditional, FunctionCall, or UserFunctionCall — they must be built manually with CompiledExprKind + content_hash computation. We need: (a) extend literal() to handle complex types, (b) add collection expression builders (list_expr, set_expr, map_expr), (c) add conditional_expr, fn_call, user_fn_call, method_call_expr, lambda_expr helpers.

3. **Field operator test helpers** — Field operations (sample, gradient, divergence, curl) are resolved as FunctionCall exprs at std::field::*. We need CompiledFieldBuilder (for CompiledField with analytical/sampled/composed/imported CompiledFieldSource variants) and field operation expression helpers that wrap fn_call.

4. **Annotated entity builders and fixtures** — CompiledModuleBuilder is missing trait_def(), field(), enum_def(), compiled_purpose() methods (it initializes these as empty vecs in build()). We need CompiledPurposeBuilder (for CompiledPurpose: params, constraints, objective, resolved_queries), CompiledTraitBuilder (for CompiledTrait: type_params, refinements, required_members as TraitRequirement, defaults as TraitDefault), the module builder extensions, and fixture modules demonstrating annotated entities.

Key types: CompiledField{name, is_pub, domain_type, codomain_type, source: CompiledFieldSource, content_hash}, CompiledPurpose{name, is_pub, params: Vec<CompiledPurposeParam>, constraints, objective, resolved_queries: Vec<ResolvedSchemaQuery>, content_hash}, CompiledTrait{name, is_pub, type_params: Vec<TypeParam>, refinements, required_members: Vec<TraitRequirement>, defaults: Vec<TraitDefault>, content_hash}, EnumDef{name, variants}. All from reify-compiler and reify-types, already dependencies.

steps.description: Write tests for complex value constructors in values.rs: int_val(42)->Value::Int(42), real_val(3.14)->Value::Real(3.14), bool_val(true)->Value::Bool(true), string_val("hi")->Value::String, enum_val("Color","Red")->Value::Enum{type_name,variant}, list_val(vec![Value::Int(1)])->Value::List, set_val(vec![Value::Int(1)])->Value::Set(BTreeSet), map_val(vec![(Value::String(..),Value::Int(..))])->Value::Map(BTreeMap), some_val(Value::Int(1))->Value::Option(Some(..)), none_val()->Value::Option(None), undef()->Value::Undef. Each test asserts the correct variant and inner contents.

Implement complex value constructors in values.rs. Add: int_val(i64)->Value::Int, real_val(f64)->Value::Real, bool_val(bool)->Value::Bool, string_val(&str)->Value::String(s.to_string()), enum_val(&str,&str)->Value::Enum{type_name,variant}, list_val(Vec<Value>)->Value::List, set_val(Vec<Value>)->Value::Set(items.into_iter().collect::<BTreeSet>()), map_val(Vec<(Value,Value)>)->Value::Map(entries.into_iter().collect::<BTreeMap>()), some_val(Value)->Value::Option(Some(Box::new(v))), none_val()->Value::Option(None), undef()->Value::Undef. Add use std::collections::{BTreeSet, BTreeMap}.

Write tests for extended literal() function in builders.rs: literal(Value::Enum{type_name:"Color",variant:"Red"}) produces CompiledExpr with result_type=Type::Enum("Color"), literal(Value::List(vec![Value::Int(1)])) produces Type::List(Box::new(Type::Int)), literal(Value::Set(btreeset)) produces Type::Set(..), literal(Value::Map(btreemap)) produces Type::Map(..), literal(Value::Option(Some(Box::new(Value::Int(1))))) produces Type::Option(Box::new(Type::Int)), literal(Value::Option(None)) produces Type::Option(Box::new(Type::Bool)) as fallback. Replace existing should_panic tests.

Extend literal() in builders.rs to handle M5 types. Remove the panic branch. Add match arms: Value::Enum{type_name,..}->Type::Enum(type_name.clone()), Value::List(v)->Type::List(Box::new(infer from first element or Type::Int)), Value::Set(s)->Type::Set(Box::new(infer from first or Type::Int)), Value::Map(m)->Type::Map(Box::new(key type), Box::new(val type)), Value::Option(Some(v))->Type::Option(Box::new(infer inner)), Value::Option(None)->Type::Option(Box::new(Type::Bool)), Value::Lambda{params,body,..}->Type::Function{params: infer from param types, return_type: body.result_type}, Value::Field{domain_type,codomain_type,..}->Type::Field{domain,codomain}. Extract helper fn infer_value_type(v:&Value)->Type for reuse.

Write tests for collection expression builders in builders.rs: list_expr(vec![literal(Value::Int(1)), literal(Value::Int(2))]) produces CompiledExprKind::ListLiteral with result_type=Type::List(Box::new(Type::Int)). set_expr(vec![literal(Value::Int(1))]) produces SetLiteral with Type::Set(Box::new(Type::Int)). map_expr(vec![(literal(Value::String(..)), literal(Value::Int(..)))]) produces MapLiteral with Type::Map(String,Int). Assert kinds and result_types.

Implement collection expression builders in builders.rs: list_expr(elements: Vec<CompiledExpr>)->CompiledExpr infers element type from first element's result_type, wraps CompiledExpr::list_literal with Type::List(Box::new(elem_type)). set_expr(elements)->CompiledExpr similarly with Type::Set. map_expr(entries: Vec<(CompiledExpr,CompiledExpr)>)->CompiledExpr infers key/val types from first entry, wraps CompiledExpr::map_literal. Panic with descriptive message if elements/entries are empty (caller must use CompiledExpr constructors directly for empty collections).

Write tests for conditional_expr and function call helpers: conditional_expr(literal(Value::Bool(true)), literal(Value::Int(1)), literal(Value::Int(2))) produces Conditional kind with result_type=Type::Int (from then_branch). fn_call("sin","std::math::sin",vec![literal(Value::Real(1.0))],Type::Real) produces FunctionCall with ResolvedFunction{name:"sin",qualified_name:"std::math::sin"}. user_fn_call("my_func",vec![literal(Value::Int(1))],Type::Int) produces UserFunctionCall.

Implement conditional_expr(condition, then_branch, else_branch)->CompiledExpr: result_type from then_branch, content_hash with tag byte [4] combined with condition+then+else hashes, kind=Conditional. fn_call(name: &str, qualified_name: &str, args: Vec<CompiledExpr>, result_type: Type)->CompiledExpr: content_hash with tag [5]+name hash+arg hashes, kind=FunctionCall{function: ResolvedFunction{name, qualified_name}}. user_fn_call(function_name: &str, args, result_type)->CompiledExpr: content_hash with tag [6]+name+args, kind=UserFunctionCall.

Write tests for method_call_expr and lambda_expr: method_call_expr(literal(Value::List(..)), "count", vec![], Type::Int) produces MethodCall{object, method:"count", args:[]}. lambda_expr(vec![("x", Type::Real)], literal(Value::Real(1.0))) produces Lambda with param_ids containing ValueCellId::new("__lambda","x"), result_type=Type::Function{params:[Type::Real], return_type: Box::new(Type::Real)}.

Implement method_call_expr(object: CompiledExpr, method: &str, args: Vec<CompiledExpr>, result_type: Type)->CompiledExpr wrapping CompiledExpr::method_call(object, method.to_string(), args, result_type). Implement lambda_expr(params: Vec<(&str, Type)>, body: CompiledExpr)->CompiledExpr: generate param_ids as ValueCellId::new("__lambda", name) for each param, build params as Vec<(String, Option<Type>)>, infer Function result_type, wrap CompiledExpr::lambda with empty captures.

Write tests for field operation expression helpers: sample_call(field_expr, point_expr, Type::Real) produces FunctionCall with function.qualified_name="std::field::sample". gradient_call(field_expr, Type::Real) produces FunctionCall with function.qualified_name="std::field::gradient" and single arg. divergence_call and curl_call similarly with their qualified names.

Implement field operation helpers in builders.rs: sample_call(field, point, result_type)->fn_call("sample","std::field::sample",vec![field,point],result_type). gradient_call(field, result_type)->fn_call("gradient","std::field::gradient",vec![field],result_type). divergence_call(field, result_type)->fn_call("divergence","std::field::divergence",vec![field],result_type). curl_call(field, result_type)->fn_call("curl","std::field::curl",vec![field],result_type).

Write tests for CompiledFieldBuilder: CompiledFieldBuilder::new("temp",Type::Geometry,Type::Real).analytical(expr).build() produces CompiledField{name:"temp",is_pub:false,source:Analytical{expr}}. .public().sampled(config).build() produces is_pub:true,source:Sampled. .composed(expr).build() and .imported().build() produce their respective source variants. Assert content_hash is non-zero for all.

Implement CompiledFieldBuilder struct{name:String, is_pub:bool, domain_type:Type, codomain_type:Type, source:Option<CompiledFieldSource>}. Methods: new(name,domain,codomain)->Self (is_pub=false, source=None), public()->Self, analytical(expr)->Self (source=Some(Analytical{expr})), sampled(config: Vec<(&str,CompiledExpr)>)->Self, composed(expr)->Self, imported()->Self, build()->CompiledField (panics if source is None, computes content_hash from name+source hash).

Write tests for CompiledPurposeBuilder: CompiledPurposeBuilder::new("mfg_ready").param("subject","Structure").constraint("subject",0,Some("thick_enough"),gt_expr).build() produces CompiledPurpose with name, 1 param (name:"subject",entity_kind:"Structure"), 1 constraint with label. .objective(Minimize{..}).schema_query("subject","params",vec![vcid]).build() adds objective and resolved_queries. Assert content_hash non-zero.

Implement CompiledPurposeBuilder struct{name, is_pub, params: Vec<CompiledPurposeParam>, constraints: Vec<CompiledConstraint>, objective: Option<OptimizationObjective>, resolved_queries: Vec<ResolvedSchemaQuery>}. Methods: new(name)->Self, public()->Self, param(name,entity_kind)->Self, constraint(entity,index,label,expr)->Self, objective(obj)->Self, schema_query(param_name,query_kind,resolved_ids)->Self, build()->CompiledPurpose with content_hash from name+constraints+queries.

Write tests for CompiledTraitBuilder: CompiledTraitBuilder::new("Rigid").require_param("thickness",Type::length()).build() produces CompiledTrait with 1 required_member (TraitRequirement{name:"thickness",kind:RequirementKind::Param(Type::length())}). .public().refinement("Bounded").require_let("vol",Type::Real).require_sub("mount","MountPoint").build() populates all fields. Assert content_hash non-zero.

Implement CompiledTraitBuilder struct{name, is_pub, type_params: Vec<TypeParam>, refinements: Vec<String>, required_members: Vec<TraitRequirement>, defaults: Vec<TraitDefault>}. Methods: new(name)->Self, public()->Self, type_param(name,bounds:Vec<TraitBound>)->Self (adds TypeParam{name,bounds,default:None}), refinement(name)->Self, require_param(name,ty)->Self (adds TraitRequirement with RequirementKind::Param(ty)), require_let(name,ty)->Self, require_sub(name,structure)->Self, build()->CompiledTrait with content_hash from name+members.

Write tests for extended CompiledModuleBuilder: CompiledModuleBuilder::new(path).trait_def(compiled_trait).field(compiled_field).enum_def(EnumDef{name:"Color",variants:vec!["Red","Blue"]}).compiled_purpose(purpose).template(tpl).build() produces CompiledModule where trait_defs.len()==1, fields.len()==1, enum_defs.len()==1, compiled_purposes.len()==1. Assert content_hash changes when these new fields are added (differs from empty module hash).

Extend CompiledModuleBuilder: add fields trait_defs: Vec<CompiledTrait>, fields: Vec<CompiledField>, enum_defs: Vec<EnumDef>, compiled_purposes: Vec<CompiledPurpose> (init empty in new()). Add methods trait_def(t)->Self, field(f)->Self, enum_def(e)->Self, compiled_purpose(p)->Self. Update build() to populate these in CompiledModule and chain their content_hashes into the combined hash.

Write tests for annotated entity fixtures: trait_structure_module() returns CompiledModule with 1 trait_def ("Rigid" requiring thickness:Length), 1 template ("Plate" with thickness param), 0 diagnostics. field_module() returns module with 1 field (analytical), 1 template. purpose_module() returns module with 1 compiled_purpose, 1 template. Assert expected counts and key field values.

Implement annotated entity fixtures in fixtures.rs using the new builders: trait_structure_module()->CompiledModule (CompiledTraitBuilder::new("Rigid").require_param("thickness",Type::length()).build() + TopologyTemplateBuilder::new("Plate") with thickness param). field_module()->CompiledModule (CompiledFieldBuilder::new("temp",..).analytical(lambda).build() + template). purpose_module()->CompiledModule (CompiledPurposeBuilder::new("mfg_ready").param("subject","Structure").constraint(..).build() + template).

design_decisions.decision: Keep all builder extensions in existing files (builders.rs, values.rs, fixtures.rs) rather than splitting into new modules
design_decisions.rationale: The crate has an established 4-module convention (builders, values, fixtures, mocks). Splitting would add complexity without clear benefit at this size. builders.rs will grow from ~550 to ~900 lines, which is manageable.

design_decisions.decision: Extend literal() to handle M5 types rather than creating a separate typed_literal() function
design_decisions.rationale: The existing panic message says 'not yet implemented', indicating the intent was always to extend it. Keeping a single entry point reduces API surface and cognitive load. Type inference uses the same logic as the compiler: infer element types from collection contents.

design_decisions.decision: Collection expression builders (list_expr, set_expr, map_expr) infer types from first element rather than requiring explicit type arguments
design_decisions.rationale: Follows the ergonomic pattern of literal() — infer when possible. In tests, collections almost always have uniform element types. For empty collections, callers use CompiledExpr::list_literal/set_literal/map_literal directly with explicit types.

design_decisions.decision: Field operation helpers (sample_call, gradient_call, etc.) delegate to fn_call() rather than constructing FunctionCall variants directly
design_decisions.rationale: Layered API: fn_call() is the general-purpose FunctionCall builder, field ops are convenient wrappers with hardcoded qualified names. This mirrors how the compiler resolves these to std::field::* functions.

design_decisions.decision: Lambda expression builder generates ValueCellIds with '__lambda' entity prefix for param IDs
design_decisions.rationale: Matches the compiler convention for lambda parameter scoping (seen as FIELD_ENTITY_PREFIX='__field' for fields). Keeps generated IDs distinct from structure-level cells.

design_decisions.decision: CompiledTraitBuilder omits default_constraint/default_param methods in initial implementation — only required_members are built
design_decisions.rationale: TraitDefault wraps syntax-level AST declarations (reify_syntax::ConstraintDecl, ParamDecl, LetDecl), which require complex manual construction. The primary test need is for trait requirements (the contract). Default handling can be added later if tests need it.

design_decisions.decision: Annotated entity fixtures use the new builders exclusively rather than manual struct construction
design_decisions.rationale: Serves as integration tests for the builder API and demonstrates intended usage patterns. If a builder can't express a fixture, that's a signal the builder API is incomplete.

design_decisions.decision: Content hash tag bytes for new expression helpers (Conditional=[4], FunctionCall=[5], UserFunctionCall=[6]) are test-only values, not matching compiler exactly
design_decisions.rationale: The test-support builders produce structurally correct but not compiler-identical hashes. The existing TopologyTemplateBuilder already follows this pattern (hash from name+children, not matching compile_structure byte-for-byte). What matters is that distinct expressions produce distinct hashes.

reuse.how: All new builders use these for content hash computation, matching the project's hash scheme
All new builders use zero-span for test convenience, same as TopologyTemplateBuilder

reuse.what: Fluent builder pattern (consume self, return Self)
reuse.how: CompiledFieldBuilder, CompiledPurposeBuilder, CompiledTraitBuilder all follow the same owned-self fluent API with terminal build()

reuse.what: CompiledExpr named constructors (literal, list_literal, set_literal, map_literal, method_call, lambda)
reuse.how: New expression builders wrap these with ergonomic type inference. list_expr wraps list_literal, set_expr wraps set_literal, etc.
Used by the extended literal() and by CompiledExpr::literal internally for computing expression content hashes
New fixtures follow same doc-comment and function-returning-CompiledModule pattern
Reused in CompiledPurposeBuilder.constraint() and existing TopologyTemplateBuilder.constraint(), same construction pattern

reuse.what: Existing expression helpers (gt, lt, ge, le, eq, ne, and, or, not, neg, binop)
reuse.how: Field operation helpers and fixture construction use these to build constraint expressions without verbose CompiledExpr construction
